key_insight: A grain-growth model trained on synthetic data can still generalize to out-of-distribution microstructures, and adding boundary-masked attention makes it more accurate by focusing on grain boundaries.
engineering_relevance: This suggests physics-informed attention can improve robustness of microstructure evolution predictors for real experimental and non-ideal materials states without retraining.
method: Tested a pre-trained deep learning grain-growth model on three OOD cases: experimental microstructures, bimodal grain size distributions, and abnormal grain growth.
Added a boundary-masked attention mechanism that restricts attention to grain-boundary pixels.
Compared baseline and physics-informed attention models without retraining or fine-tuning on OOD data.
evidence_signal: Across all three OOD cases, both models generalized successfully, with the largest gain in the bimodal case where SSIM improved from 0.6221 to 0.7609 and mean grain-size error dropped from 8.75% to 3.57%.
watch_item: The improvement appears strongest when boundary morphology is similar to the training domain, so performance may be less predictable for highly novel microstructures.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Attention Mechanism and Generalization Capability of Deep Learning-Based Grain Growth Evolution Prediction
